summary: First test run for MHS-ScaleOps landing page. Backend status endpoints (GET /api/, POST /api/status, GET /api/status) all pass with proper validation and no _id leakage. Frontend renders all sections correctly: navbar logo, hero CTAs (calendly+jotform), 4 stat cards, offer section with 12 deliverables, 3 phases, naixol IG link, 4 roadmap weeks, FAQ accordion (3 items verified expand), calendly iframe lazy-loaded on scroll, final CTA links, footer (email/calendly/instagram + 5 nav anchors), smooth-scroll, and mobile menu toggle at 390px. Zero console errors.
critical_code_review_comments: App.js uses BrowserRouter with a single '/' route — fine for an SPA landing page.
Calendly iframe is lazy-loaded via IntersectionObserver — good performance pattern.
Backend status endpoint correctly excludes MongoDB _id (find with projection {_id:0}).
All interactive elements have data-testid attributes as required.
Hero uses an external Pexels image — not blocking but consider self-hosting for reliability.
seed_data_creation: Created 2 TEST_-prefixed status check rows via POST /api/status during pytest run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Landing page is fully functional. Backend has only the boilerplate /api/ + /api/status endpoints. Pytest file at /app/backend/tests/test_status_api.py covers them. All 18 review_request items verified and passing.
